title: cystium scorpionis
intro: Cystium scorpionis, also known as bladderwort, is a carnivorous aquatic plant native to North America and Europe. The plant's leaves are covered with bladder-like traps that capture small organisms and insects for nutrition. The medicinal properties of Cystium scorpionis include its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms associated with indigestion and bloating. Herbal preparations of the plant, typically in the form of tea or tinctures, have been traditionally used to promote digestion and alleviate nausea, with potential benefits also seen in reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health.
